title: Edi Wedd

{{Quote|It was foolish to attack my fortress, Jedi.|Edi Wedd to Bultar Swan|<ref name="imp" />}}
[[Baron/Legends|Baron]] '''Edi Wedd''' was a decorated [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. A proud and arrogant Separatist sympathizer, Wedd was unsuccessfully indicted by their people for directing resources to the Confederate government. Joining the Separatist cause, the Baron constructed a massive, impenetrable [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] called the [[Impregnable (fortress)|Impregnable]] on [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Amaltanna/Legends|Amaltanna]], where they mercilessly eradicated a sieging force composed of their own people. 

When [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|forces]] met those of Wedd at the [[Battle of Amaltanna/Legends|Battle of Amaltanna]], the Baron remained inside the fortress, safeguarded from invasion. A furious battle ensued, resulting in a total loss of all combatants save for [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]], Wedd, and the [[Battle droid/Legends|droids]] kept inside the colossal stronghold. Swan thereafter entered the Impregnable and advanced toward the multi-[[Limb/Legends|limbed]] Separatist commander as they taunted her over [[Speaker/Legends|loudspeakers]]. Wedd activated the defense systems remotely, but the relentless [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] dispatched the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and evaded the traps, deliberately impairing the [[Communications relay/Legends|communications relay]] in the process. When she found Wedd's innermost sanctum, the insectoid remained inside, proudly extolling its impenetrability; Swan simply destroyed the door's control panel and life-support systems, encasing Baron Wedd within the Impregnable fortress forever.

==Biography==
===Separatist sympathizer===
{{Quote|My own people attempted to put me on trial for sending resources to the Separatists. It took some time to kill them all, but my new servants are just as expendable.|Edi Wedd to Bultar Swan|<ref name="imp" />}}
[[File:Impregnable_fortress.png|thumb|270px|right|Baron Wedd erected the Impregnable on Amaltanna.]]
Edi Wedd<ref name="cswe" /> was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] who [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from a [[Planet/Legends|world]] otherwise unaffiliated with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]],<ref name="imp" /> a Separatist government at [[Clone Wars/Legends|war]] with the millennia-old [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="aotc">{{Film|II}}</ref> Some time prior to the [[Battle of Amaltanna/Legends|Battle of Amaltanna]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Wedd began to sympathize with the Separatists and directed resources thereto; their people unsuccessfully attempted to have them tried for these acts.<ref name="imp" /> At some point, the insectoid acquired the title of [[Baron/Legends|Baron]]<ref name="cswe" /> and became the decorated [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of a Separatist force.<ref name="imp" />

As a Separatist leader, Wedd established a virtually impenetrable [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] on planet [[Amaltanna/Legends|Amaltanna]], which was aptly christened the [[Impregnable (fortress)|Impregnable]]. The stronghold was constructed of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] of [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] surrounded by [[Force field/Legends|force fields]] which were capable of repelling even [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]].<ref name="imp" /> The Baron took up residence inside the Impregnable<ref name="cswe" /> and eradicated an [[Army/Legends|army]] of their own people in a lengthy battle as they besieged the fortress.<ref name="imp" />

===Death on Amaltanna===
{{Quote|This fortress cannot be breached! There is no way in...and no way out.|Edi Wedd admits their doom|<ref name="imp" />}}
[[File:Battle_of_Amaltanna.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Swan poses on a LAAT gunship in the aftermath of the battle.]]
In 22 BBY,<ref name="chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> six months after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]<ref name="imp" /> sparked the Clone Wars,<ref name="aotc" /> Wedd's fortress became the site of a destructive engagement between the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] and the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] of the Separatists. The two forces clashed with ferocity; as the battle ended, the scene was quite desolate. All units had been lost save for the Republic [[General/Legends|leader]]—[[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]]—as well as Wedd, who remained safely inside their fortress with a collection of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. As Swan approached the imposing structure, Wedd monitored<ref name="imp" /> her<ref name="swandatabank">{{DB|character|bultarswan|Swan, Bultar}}</ref> movements through a [[Camera/Legends|camera]] system and offered to accept her surrender over the [[Speaker/Legends|loudspeakers]].<ref name="imp" />

Swan, undeterred, ignored the offer and proceeded into the stronghold, where Wedd continued to scoff at her intrusion. From their inner sanctum, they activated wall-mounted [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weaponry and feigned affection towards the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] for her single-minded pursuit of them. She responded by deflecting the attack and resuming her advance. The Baron then expressed their objection to life [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and recounted their willingness to annihilate their own people out of self-interest. As the intruder proceeded to a ledge which bridged a deep chasm within the facility, Wedd deployed a swarm of [[Unidentified spider droid|spider droids]] to overwhelm the Jedi while she was perched precariously thereover. Swan, however, was not so easily outclassed; she finished the droids and leapt to the opposite deck, avoiding the retracting walkway.<ref name="imp" />

[[File:Wedd_frightened.png|thumb|right|270px|The Baron anxiously calls after Swan as she departs their chamber.]]
Wedd continued to sneer at her success, invoking the Impregnable's labyrinthine design and asking Swan if she had lost her way when she entered its [[Communications relay/Legends|communications array]]. Still in remote control of the facility, Wedd ambushed the Jedi there with a company of [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|super battle droids]], but she made short work of these units and irreparably damaged the communications equipment on her way out. Finally, she approached the innermost chamber<ref name="imp" /> in which Wedd lived.<ref name="cswe" /> The Separatist declared victory, noting that the solitary entrance was [[Magnetic seal/Legends|magnetically sealed]] and too thick to be sliced by [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. The Jedi silently slashed through the door's control mechanism<ref name="imp" /> and the facility's life-support systems,<ref name="cswe" /> destroying them, and departed nonchalantly. An exasperated Wedd announced that Swan had thereby isolated herself from any hope of capturing them. Wedd then came to realize her true intention—she had entombed the Baron within their proud, inaccessible fortress.<ref name="imp" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Tell me, did you think I would simply come with you? That I would accept life imprisonment on Coruscant? Typical Jedi arrogance.|Edi Wedd|<ref name="imp" />}}
[[File:Wedd_frowning.png|thumb|250px|left|Wedd's fate was sealed through their own miscalculations.]]
Baron Wedd was a [[Color/Legends|green]] insectoid being who possessed at least six ciliate [[Eye/Legends|eye]]s and a [[Tooth/Legends|toothed]] [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]. They also maintained six pointed [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], which extended in groups of three from each of their two shoulders. In military attire, the Baron wore a black [[Suit/Legends|suit]] displaying [[Military rank/Legends|military regalia]] over white-and-red [[Clothing/Legends|undergarments]]. They also sported yellow [[Epaulet/Legends|epaulets]] as well as a tall, cylindrical [[Hat/Legends|hat]] adorned with insignia.<ref name="imp" />

Baron Wedd was a proud [[Alien/Legends|alien]] who displayed no qualms in dissenting from popular opinion against the Separatist cause. Indeed, the Separatist commander retained no allegiance to former compatriots, displaying even genocidal tendencies when they massacred a force comprised of their former people. Wedd was quite proud of the Impregnable fortress, boasting of its enormity and considerable defenses and smugly dismissing the possibility of invasion by their enemies. As a military mind, Wedd experienced success at least twice when utterly annihilating both forces that attempted to assail it. Wedd was also prone to some amount of verbosity and gave a lengthy, sarcastic speech to Swan as she advanced toward Wedd's central chamber. However, their lack of forethought as to the Impregnable's design—as well as misjudgments concerning Swan's exploitation thereof—resulted in Wedd's demise within the mighty structure.<ref name="imp" />

==Behind the scenes==
Baron Edi Wedd first appeared as an unidentified insectoid Separatist commander in ''[[Impregnable (comic story)|Impregnable]]'', a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] collected in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 7]]'', written by [[Chris Avellone]] and illustrated by [[Ethan Beavers]] and [[Dan Jackson]]. ''Impregnable'' was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 31]], [[2007]].<ref name="imp" /><ref name="darkhorse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/14-333/Star-Wars-Clone-Wars-Adventures-Volume-7|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 7}}</ref> Wedd was then given a name in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe" />
